records.question_text:
Wines 4 to 6 are made from the same grape variety.
For all three wines:
a) Identify the grape variety (15 marks)
For each wine:
b) Identify the origin as closely as possible and comment on the level of quality within the context of that origin (3 x 15 marks)
c) Discuss how the wine has been made, with specific reference to the use of oak and malolactic fermentation (3 x 5 marks)

Vouvray Clos du Bourg Demi-Sec, Domaine Huet, 2022. Loire, France. (13%)

records.question_text:
Wines 1-3 are from the same single grape variety or predominant grape variety.

With reference to all three wines:

a) Identify the grape variety. (12 marks)

For each wine:

b) Identify the origin as closely as possible. (3 x 6 marks)

c) Discuss how climate and winemaking techniques have influenced the quality and style of the wine. (3 x 15 marks)

wines.wine_text: Chinon Les Barnabés, Olga Raffault, 2021. Loire Valley, France. (12.5%)
Cabernet Franc, El Enemigo, 2021. Mendoza, Argentina. (13.5%)
Toscana IGT “Aranum”, Tenuta di Arceno, 2018. Tuscany, Italy. (14.5%)

records.question_text:
Wines 1 to 3 are made from the same single grape variety.

a) Identify the grape variety with reference to all three wines. (12 marks)

For each wine:

b) Identify the origin as closely as possible. (3 x 7 marks)

c) Comment on the winemaking techniques. (3 x 5 marks)

d) Comment on the style and quality. (3 x 9 marks)

wines.wine_text: Riesling Sekt Extra Dry, Dr. Loosen, NV. Mosel, Germany. (11.5%)
Riesling, Framingham, 2023. Marlborough, New Zealand. (12%)
Riesling Beerenauslese, Dr. Loosen, 2018. Mosel, Germany. (7.5%)

records.question_text:
Wines 4 to 6 are from the same country.

For each wine:

a) Identify the origin as closely as possible. (3 x 10 marks)

b) Comment on the winemaking techniques. (3 x 5 marks)

c) Comment on the quality and commercial position. (3 x 10 marks)

wines.wine_text: Cava Brut Reserva “Essential Púrpura”, Juvé y Camps, 2021. Penedès, Spain. (12%)
La Bruja, Comando G, 2022. Valle del Tiétar, Sierra de Gredos, Spain. (14%)
Oloroso VORS 30 Years, Bodegas Tradición, NV. Jerez, Spain. (20%)

records.question_text:
Wines 7 to 12 are a mixed bag.

For each wine:

a) Comment on the quality and commercial position with specific reference to its classification. (6 x 15 marks)

b) Comment on the winemaking techniques. (6 x 10 marks)

wines.wine_text: Côte de Provence Cru Classé, Chateau Galoupet, 2023. Provence, France. (14%)
Pinot Gris Spiegel Grand Cru, Schlumberger, 2022. Alsace, France. (13.5%)
Tokaji Édes Szamorodni, István Szepsy, 2017. Tokaji, Hungary. (12%)
Sercial 10 Years Old, Henriques & Henriques, NV. Madeira, Portugal. (20%)
Fine Ruby Port, Cockburns, NV. Douro, Portugal. (19%)
40 Year Tawny Port, Kopke, NV. Douro, Portugal. (20%)

mock_v6_p1_q2.question_text:
Wines 5-7 are all made from the same single grape variety and come from the same country.

With reference to all three wines:

a) Identify the grape variety and country of origin. (15 marks)

For each wine:

b) Identify the specific origin as closely as possible. (3 x 7 marks)

c) Rank the wines in quality order, justifying your ranking with reference to origin, site expression, producer reputation and regional hierarchy. (24 marks)

d) Comment on the commercial positioning of each wine. (3 x 5 marks)

wines.wine_text: Sancerre Les Romains, Henri Bourgeois, 2024. Loire Valley, France. (13%)
Menetou-Salon Les Bornés, Domaine de Chasseignes, 2024. Loire Valley, France. (12.5%)
Pouilly-Fumé Les Berthiers, Jean-Claude Dagueneau, 2023. Loire Valley, France. (13%)
